Global (All).title: Global Cyberattacks on Ukraine Infrastructure Disrupts Critical Services
Global (All).summary: A series of debilitating cyberattacks hit Ukraine's critical infrastructure, affecting power systems, communication networks, and other vital services.

Global (All).title: International Tensions Escalate as Russia-Ukraine Conflict Enters Critical Phase
Global (All).summary: The prolonged conflict between Russia and Ukraine has taken a catastrophic turn, with both sides engaging in aggressive military maneuvers, escalating global tensions.

Global (All).title: Climate Change Protesters Block International Flights in Major Airports Worldwide
Global (All).summary: Climate change protesters staged a massive global action, blocking international flights and disrupting air travel in major airports, drawing international attention to the urgent need for climate action.

United States.summary: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky says that Ukraine has 'no other option' but to negotiate with Russia as the war rages on. The conflict has led to increased tensions between the US and Russia.
The FBI issues a warning to US businesses, warning them about a potential cyberattack threat from a hacking group called 'DarkSide'. The group has been linked to recent ransomware attacks.
The US House of Representatives passes a bipartisan resolution denouncing a recent surge in hate crimes against Asian Americans, and calling for action to prevent violence.

South Korea.title: North Korea says it fired ballistic missiles into the sea off its east coast, amid concerns over a potential seventh nuclear test.
South Korea.summary: The launches, which Seoul's military said occurred on July 18, come as international pressure builds over a possible nuclear test by Pyongyang, which has not conducted such an explosion in years.

South Korea.title: South Korea and the US stage joint drills near the Demilitarized Zone with North Korea, despite a warning from Pyongyang that this could trigger conflict.
South Korea.summary: The exercises, which took place in the Gyeonggi Province just south of the Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) that separates the two Koreas, involved thousands of troops and involved simulated battles against an enemy force.

South Korea.title: South Korean police fire water cannons and smoke bombs to disperse protesters in Incheon as demonstrations over a major labor dispute turn violent.
South Korea.summary: The clashes, which occurred on July 15, marked the latest escalation in a long-running dispute between employers and workers at a major logistics center, with the two sides still negotiating a resolution.

Japan.title: Japan's Kishida Warns of Global Threat from China
Japan.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Fumio Kishida warned about the global threat posed by China, citing its increasing military activities and aggressive behavior in the Asia-Pacific region.

Japan.title: Japan's Economy Shrank for Second Straight Quarter in 2023 Q2
Japan.summary: Japan's economy shrank for the second consecutive quarter in 2023's quarter two, due to weak exports and a decline in business sentiment.

Japan.title: Japanese Prime Minister Kishida Set for Re-election as Party Leader
Japan.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Fumio Kishida's bid for re-election as leader of the ruling Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) is gaining momentum, but faces opposition from other party members who want to see him replaced.
